items.quote: Only James Dean and Rachael Coad are on record as contact roles despite 15+ known account-level contacts (e.g. Group Procurement Director, Group Digital Transformation Director).

metrics.narrative: Rachael Coad quantified the scale of the pain on the 2026-04-10 call: indirect spend ~£650M with ~30% (~£195M) untracked tail spend across 2,000 live suppliers, direct spend ~£2B, and an £80M synergy target from the Bakavol acquisition that is 'not linked to any solutions at the moment' but which she said an S2P system 'would really help' with. SF's Customer_Business_Problem__c corroborates this (unmanaged ~30% tail spend, contract leakage, no PO visibility). No formal ROI/business-case model has yet been confirmed delivered back to her despite Anthony Gray offering to send one after the 10 Apr call — closing that loop is the move to convert quantified pain into a defensible business case.

champion.narrative: Rachael Coad is a confirmed, strong champion: SF Next_Step_History__c logged by the deal owner on 2026-05-12 explicitly states 'Champion Established in Rachael Coad'. Her own words on the 10 Apr call back this up: 'I will be running the project, and it looks like at the moment I will be owning the solution... I'm deeply invested. I think there's such an opportunity to make life better... I'm like a big fan.' James Dean is a secondary, more passive co-lead working alongside her ('James and I will be working on this together'), confirmed to attend the Zycus procurement/EY event in October 2026 to deepen the relationship in a peer setting — good but not yet as engaged or vocal as Rachael.

competition.narrative: No external S2P rival has been named yet — the competitive picture today is entirely against weak, disconnected legacy point tools: Scanmarket (eProcurement), DocuSign (contract repository), and Click (spend visibility, per SF Zycus_Differentiation_Why_Zycus__c and the 10 Apr call), all described by Rachael as standalone and easy to displace. The one embedded tool with real retention risk is Pactum, an AI autonomous-negotiation tool Greencore has used for two years ('would I have said that we've got an AI autonomous negotiation tool? I'd have laughed in your face... we're in our second year of having it now'), plus a separate, unnamed AI contract-redlining tool she says she 'loves'. Since Rachael plans an RFP only toward end of 2026, the real competitive shortlist has not yet formed; Zycus currently holds an influence position shaping her business case ahead of that formal process.

identify_pain.narrative: Extensive, specific pain was articulated by Rachael Coad on the 10 Apr call: 7 disparate ERP systems (legacy Infor System 21, Great Plains, Microsoft Dynamics 365) with a 5-year migration to SAP S/4HANA; standalone Scanmarket (eProcurement), DocuSign (contract repository, 'doesn't tie into anything'), and Click (spend visibility, 'works well for directs, not well for indirects'); zero visibility into purchase orders ('I've never seen a purchase order'); a cited example of a site buying engineering-resource services from a bolts supplier due to lack of controls; and historic contract auto-renewals from an unmanaged SharePoint folder. SF's Customer_Business_Problem__c corroborates this as fragmented systems, poor spend visibility, unmanaged tail spend, and reactive/manual AP. Rachael is the clear pain owner as the procurement technology lead.

paper_process.narrative: No contracting mechanics have been discussed; the deal is pre-RFP and pre-budget so MSA/procurement-channel/legal review has not yet come up on any call or field. This is expected at this stage (Qualified, long-play) rather than a red flag. Visibility will come once Rachael's business case is approved and a formal RFP/vendor-selection process launches, which she has stated targets end of 2026.

economic_buyer.narrative: No named economic buyer is mapped (Executive_Sponsor_Identified__c = false, X10a_Sponsor__c blank). Rachael Coad was explicit on the 2026-04-10 call that she does not control the funding decision: 'The business isn't quite as enthusiastic as me yet, and part of my job... is being able to create a really compelling business case that I can go to the business with... and then we can just press the button and go ahead.' She named that 'once we've got business case sign off, a whole team will be put together.' The evidence shows the budget owner is unnamed senior leadership sitting above Rachael; mapping that person is a live gap.

decision_process.narrative: The process was laid out clearly by Rachael Coad on the 10 Apr call: build the business case first, present to the business for budget sign-off, then run a go-to-market/RFP process ('I don't want to go to market first, there is a lot of work to be done in terms of requirement gathering... we are under an incredible amount of time pressure'). SF Next_Step_History__c confirms this is being executed as a deliberate long-play: discovery (10 Apr) -> demo (15 May) -> continued warm nurture -> RFP toward end of 2026. Compelling_Event__c corroborates the Bakavol-driven MBE transformation program and planned SAP S/4HANA migration as the backdrop forcing this timeline.

decision_criteria.narrative: Rachael Coad set explicit demo criteria on the 10 Apr call: 'It has to be interactive. It must be live... give us specific use cases' and named four focus areas — AI capabilities, savings-opportunity analysis, contract management, and supplier risk management (also captured in SF as the requirements-from-Zycus note). Zycus offered an RFP primer document specifically to help her formalize a fuller requirements list ahead of any RFP; no evidence yet that formal decision criteria beyond the demo brief have been documented.

engagement.top_event: Three explicit deliverables (UK food references, ECC6 integration plan, Merlin ROI model) unaddressed and due before BRD fit session on 17 June. These are table stakes for Vendor Selected gate.

Zycus is shaping the RFP itself via the primer sent 18 May 2026, a strong influence position ahead of the end-2026 RFP; convert that into named must-have criteria (agentic AI, tail-spend automation, one-data-core architecture) before the buyer finalizes scope.
best_practice_check.summary: This is a long-play, single-threaded deal where the strongest lever available right now is deepening influence before Rachael's RFP forms in late 2026 — not chasing a false close date.

competitive_position.summary: No external S2P vendor is currently in a formal bake-off — the competitive picture is entirely against Greencore's own weak, disconnected legacy point tools. Scanmarket (eProcurement) and DocuSign (contract repository) are the clearest, lowest-threat displacement targets per Rachael Coad's own words ('doesn't tie into anything'). Click (spend visibility) is a secondary legacy tool in the same category. The one real retention risk is Pactum, an embedded AI autonomous-negotiation tool in its second year of use, plus a separate unnamed AI contract-redlining tool Rachael says she 'loves' — both could be defended by the buyer as 'already working' unless Zycus demonstrates clear superiority. Since Rachael's own stated plan is to issue a formal RFP only toward end of 2026, the true competitive shortlist has not yet formed; all current evidence is dated 2026-04-10.

deal_scores_evidence.summary: An early-stage, pre-RFP relationship with a genuinely enthusiastic and articulate champion and strong documented pain, held back by an unmapped economic buyer, a stated resourcing blocker on the buyer side, and two open Zycus commitments that are overdue with no confirmation of delivery.

deal_risk.text: The Salesforce close date (12 Apr 2026) is already 88 days in the past against a Qualified-stage, pre-RFP deal and needs resetting to reflect the buyer's stated end-2026 RFP timeline.
No economic buyer or budget owner is named; the business case and internal funding approval have not been secured.
Entrenched, buyer-loved point solutions (Pactum, an unnamed AI redlining tool) could survive any eventual selection and narrow Zycus's scope.
Single-threaded relationship (2 contacts) creates exposure if Rachael's stated bandwidth constraints cause the initiative to be deprioritised internally.

win_position.text: Champion Rachael Coad is highly self-motivated and told Zycus on 10 Apr 2026 she is 'deeply invested' and expects to personally own the solution.
Pain is extensive and quantified: seven ERP systems, 80% of 2,000 indirect suppliers untracked in tail spend, and zero procurement visibility into purchase orders (10 Apr 2026 call).
No economic buyer is identified or engaged; budget depends on an unnamed senior leadership audience Rachael has not yet secured (10 Apr 2026 call).
The deal is single-threaded on two contacts (Rachael Coad, James Dean) with no confirmed reach into IT, finance, or wider senior leadership.
Rachael already praises two installed AI point tools (Pactum for negotiation, an unnamed redlining tool), which could narrow Zycus's eventual win scope unless positioned as full consolidation.

deal_momentum.text: A genuine two-way buyer email reply landed on 2 Jun 2026 and further Salesforce activity was logged on 23 Jun 2026, keeping the relationship inside the last 30-40 days.
The only content-rich touchpoint on record is the 15 May 2026 on-site demo (about eight weeks ago); nothing confirms what, if anything, has moved since.
Two Zycus commitments from the 10 Apr 2026 call (an RFP primer and a savings/business-case model) remain unconfirmed as delivered 13 weeks later.

The buyer has explicitly framed this as 'a long play deal' with the RFP not expected until end of 2026, so the quiet stretch since May is consistent with the stated cadence rather than an unexplained stall.

customer_commitment.text: Rachael Coad described herself as 'deeply invested' and a self-identified fan of the Zycus vision on the 10 Apr 2026 call.
Rachael explicitly flagged she lacks the internal headcount/bandwidth to run an implementation programme at the 15 May 2026 on-site demo — the stated primary blocker to progression.
James Dean's engagement so far is limited to attending the demo and a follow-on Zycus event; no direct quote from him is on record.

hard.ais_why:
• The SF Opportunity Description records Greencore's strategic context: 'The organization is currently undergoing a strategic transformation program called Making Business Easier (MBE), aimed at modernizing its supply chain, improving operational efficiency, and enabling data-driven decision-making across the enterprise.' Following the Bakkavor acquisition reaching ~¬£4B revenue, Greencore has 'significantly expanded its scale... increased operational complexity and accelerated the need for digital transformation.' Full Zycus AI suite in scope (ANA, Merlin, Merlin Intake) aligned to this MBE program.
• Qualified-stage with recurring April 2026 working sessions and a scheduled 15 May 2026 meeting attended by Greencore stakeholders James Dean and Rachael Coad with Zycus team (Anthony Gray, Shriya Iyer, Parama Sarkar). BD_Engagement is Unengaged in SFDC but the multi-party meeting cadence suggests active discovery underway. The data-driven decision-making framing from the Description aligns naturally with Merlin Insights and ANA positioning.
• Confidence is Medium because the SF Description provides a strong strategic context but the Avoma summary for this opp returned content from an unrelated account (Avoma fuzzy-match error), so we lack verbatim customer-side AI quotes. The score relies on the Description's explicit transformation language and the agentic/Merlin product mix. A confirmed AI champion or RFP issuance would move this to 8+.
